records.ayah_level_surprise: Pearls and coral emerge from the paired waters before towering ships appear on the sea; immediately afterward, everyone upon the earth is said to perish. Valuable natural yield and monumental craft are thus placed together under divine possession and against human finitude.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000086/B009` (sea travel and relation) is retained. It makes the target contribute a navigable domain and a relation of maritime movement, not merely a location surrounding the ships.
records.activation_trace: ٱلْجَوَارِ is a moving-vessel designation, ٱلْمُنشَـَٔاتُ identifies the vessels as raised or constructed, and فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ locates their motion directly in the target. These cues visibly realize the sea-travel branch.
records.negative_evidence: كَٱلْأَعْلَٰمِ magnifies the ships, not the lexical breadth of بَحْر, so it does not independently activate `root_000086/B002`. No other branch has a local trigger.

records.primary_reading: The plural seas are presented as undergoing the catastrophic passive event سُجِّرَتْ within a chain of end-time reversals.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Prized she-camels are abandoned, wild animals are gathered, seas are transformed, souls are paired, and the buried girl is questioned. The sea's upheaval belongs to a sequence in which ordinary classifications and controls collapse so that moral accountability can come forward.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: The repeated وَإِذَا plus passive verbs creates a tight sequence of imposed transformations. That sequence changes the seas' narrative role, but سُجِّرَتْ does not visibly cue saltiness, cleaving, travel, breadth, or another accepted ب ح ر branch.
records.negative_evidence: The plural form alone is not evidence for `root_000086/B002` (expanse), and catastrophic alteration alone does not establish `root_000086/B003` (wide cleft). No secondary branch passes the standard.

records.primary_reading: The seas are water bodies made to burst open or break forth as part of the cosmic dissolution.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Sky, stars, seas, and graves undergo parallel disassembly before a self knows what it sent ahead and left behind. The marine rupture is therefore one scale in a sequence that moves from the cosmos to personal disclosure.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000086/B003` (wide cleft) is retained. It adds a boundary-breaking image to the water body: the seas contribute not only released water but the opening of what had contained or divided it.
records.activation_trace: The target's own passive فُجِّرَتْ is a direct rupture cue. It is reinforced locally by ٱنفَطَرَتْ for the sky, ٱنتَثَرَتْ for the stars, and بُعْثِرَتْ for the graves. The repeated opening and scattering supplies an abductive bridge from bursting seas to the accepted wide-cleft branch.
records.negative_evidence: No animal-ear or bahirah referent is imported from `root_000086/B003`; only its cleft image resonates. The window gives no cue for saltiness, travel, bewilderment, or the root's single-source bodily and medical branches.

records.primary_reading: Moses seeks water on behalf of his people; the following command and twelve issuing springs constitute the answer to that request.
records.ayah_level_surprise: A need for drink is answered through a struck stone with twelve differentiated springs, so relief arrives together with social order. The next ayah's dissatisfaction with one food places this abundant, apportioned answer against a renewed demand for variety.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B006` (prayer for watering or rain) and `root_000722/B002` (providing a watering source) are retained. B006 makes the request intercessory address, while B002 makes its object durable access to sources rather than one immediate drink.
records.activation_trace: مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ and the answering فَقُلْنَا visibly frame the request as prayer on others' behalf, activating B006. ٱثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا followed by كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ مَّشْرَبَهُمْ supplies persistent, group-differentiated water access, directly activating B002. The bridge is the movement from petition to provisioned infrastructure.
records.negative_evidence: The springs are for drinking, not explicitly irrigation, so `root_000722/B003` is not retained; the crop list in 2:61 belongs to a new demand and does not establish an irrigation share in the focus ayah. No cloud, bodily fluid, dye, or heart-forcing cue appears.

records.primary_reading: مَّشْرَبَهُمْ denotes each group's recognized drinking place or source.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The singular possessive allotted to every group stands beside twelve springs and the universal كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ. A miraculous abundance is made legible as differentiated access, not an undirected mass of water.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B002` (allotted share of water) and `root_000783/B014` (watered land or green growth) are retained. B002 adds an apportioned entitlement to the locative sense; B014 extends the drinking source toward the land's productive water-dependence visible in the next ayah.
records.activation_trace: The counted ٱثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا, distributive كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ, possessive مَّشْرَبَهُمْ, and cognition marker قَدْ عَلِمَ jointly activate B002. For B014, the immediate continuation مِمَّا تُنۢبِتُ ٱلْأَرْضُ followed by a concrete crop list is a visible green-growth cue; it lets the assigned drinking source resonate as part of the water-fed ecology from which food is requested.
records.negative_evidence: B014 supplements rather than converts مَّشْرَب into farmland; the target remains a drinking place. Drinkers as a social group (`root_000783/B003`) is not separately retained because كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ marks recipients, not a lexical co-drinking relation.

records.primary_reading: The imperative commands the people to drink from God's provision alongside the command to eat.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Permission to consume is immediately bounded by a prohibition against فساد in the earth, and the next ayah shows desire turning from received provision toward substitution. Drinking is therefore presented as grateful use subject to conduct, not bare satisfaction.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B002` (allotted share of water) is retained. It makes the act of drinking participation in an ordered allocation rather than indiscriminate access.
records.activation_trace: Immediately before the imperative, twelve springs are counted and every group is said to know مَّشْرَبَهُمْ. That explicit distributive arrangement supplies the branch cue; the bridge is that the commanded act draws from each group's known share.
records.negative_evidence: مِن رِّزْقِ ٱللَّهِ and the eat-drink pairing enrich the ethics of consumption but do not state the wealth-spending idiom required for `root_000783/B012`. No color absorption, rope, bodily-part, or understanding construction changes this literal imperative.

records.primary_reading: The passive causative depicts the calf as having been imbued into their hearts through disbelief: an attachment has permeated the inner seat of response.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The command to take revelation firmly and listen is answered by سَمِعْنَا وَعَصَيْنَا, after which the calf is described as what actually enters the heart. Hearing and inward uptake split apart: the rejected command remains external while the idol becomes internal.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B011` (absorbing as understanding) and `root_000783/B008` (putting a rope around the neck) are retained. B011 supplies an ironic cognition contrast: they hear without truly taking in the command, yet take in the calf. B008 adds a tethering image in which the internalized calf becomes a binding attachment.
records.activation_trace: For B011, the exact sequence وَٱسْمَعُوا۟ / سَمِعْنَا / وَعَصَيْنَا visibly stages failed hear-and-understand uptake. For B008, ٱلْعِجْلَ is the explicit animal cue, while أَخَذْنَا مِيثَٰقَكُمْ, خُذُوا۟, and بِقُوَّةٍ create a repeated holding and binding frame. The bridge is not that the heart becomes a literal neck, but that calf-attachment functions like a tether under the branch's attested rope image.
records.negative_evidence: The primary permeation already corresponds to the physical and affective absorption image of `root_000783/B007`, so it is not counted as an additional secondary resonance. The accusation, neck-craning, vessel, and water-share branches have no visible local trigger.

records.primary_reading: The imperative permits drinking during the fasting night until dawn becomes distinguishable, after which the fast is completed until night.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The permission is embedded between divine nearness and response in 2:186, repentance within 2:187, and prohibitions on consuming wealth in 2:188. Bodily permission is therefore neither isolated indulgence nor mere scheduling; it belongs to a responsive, forgiven, and ethically bounded life.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B002` (allotted share of water) and `root_000783/B012` (wealth consumed in feeding and watering) are retained. B002 turns the drink into a temporally allotted portion; B012 makes the lawful eat-drink pair resonate against the immediately following unlawful consumption of wealth.
records.activation_trace: For B002, حَتَّىٰ, the white/black thread contrast, dawn, ثُمَّ, night, and حُدُودُ ٱللَّهِ visibly measure the permitted turn for drinking; 2:185's عِدَّة and 2:189's مَوَٰقِيت reinforce that allotment frame. For B012, وَكُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ is followed immediately by وَلَا تَأْكُلُوٓا۟ أَمْوَٰلَكُم and a second لِتَأْكُلُوا۟ ... أَمْوَٰلِ ٱلنَّاسِ. That exact consumption-and-wealth sequence bridges the branch to the target, contrasting licensed nourishment with illicit resource consumption.
records.negative_evidence: The repeated بيان language governs recognition of limits but does not make ٱشْرَبُوا itself mean understanding, so `root_000783/B011` is not retained. No vessel, drinker-group, unpleasant-water, absorption, rope, or watered-land cue changes this target.

records.primary_reading: The singular verb denotes actually drinking from the river; that ordinary act is the stated test of allegiance.
records.ayah_level_surprise: A mundane bodily act sorts the army before battle. The surrounding claims about fitness for kingship (2:247), the sign of the ark (2:248), and the victory of a small band (2:250–251) make restraint at water an embodied qualification for belonging and rule.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B002` (an allotted share of water) is retained as a secondary resonance. The verb still means drinking, but the permitted handful makes drinking answerable to a defined portion.
records.activation_trace: The local exception `إِلَّا مَنِ ٱغْتَرَفَ غُرْفَةً بِيَدِهِ` supplies an explicitly bounded quantity, while `مُبْتَلِيكُم بِنَهَرٍ` places it inside a test. That quantity activates B002 as the role of legitimate allotment; the bridge is that the difference between failure and permission is not liquid versus no liquid but unbounded drinking versus the allowed share. The target consequently contributes measured reception, not thirst-satisfaction alone.
records.negative_evidence: The army and the river create broad social and locative imagery, but this singular occurrence does not itself name co-drinkers (B003) or a drinking place (B004). No other branch passes the evidence standard.

records.primary_reading: The plural verb reports that the troops drank from the river, with only a few excepted.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The mass act reverses the expected value of abundance: the many drink and falter, while the few who endure become the victorious company. The following prayer for poured-out patience (`أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا`) further contrasts bodily intake with divinely supplied steadfastness.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B003` (drinkers and co-drinkers as a group) and `root_000783/B002` (an allotted share of water) are retained. The plural drinking becomes both a social classification and a violation of the visibly bounded allowance.
records.activation_trace: For B003, `بِٱلْجُنُودِ`, `فَشَرِبُوا`, `إِلَّا قَلِيلًا مِّنْهُمْ`, and the later contrast `فِئَةٍ قَلِيلَةٍ`/`فِئَةً كَثِيرَةً` repeatedly sort collective bodies; the branch supplies the role of a drinking-defined company, so the target helps constitute which group can continue with Talut. For B002, the prior `غُرْفَةً بِيَدِهِ` is the permitted share; the bridge is the direct sequence from allowance to the plural report, making their drinking register as excess over an allotment rather than consumption in the abstract.
records.negative_evidence: Nothing describes water quality, absorption, a vessel, or another specialized branch. B003 and B002 exhaust the visible root-local cues.

records.primary_reading: `شَرَابِكَ` is the man's drink or beverage, preserved without the aging expected over a hundred years.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The drink is not merely provision: alongside food, the ass, and reassembled bones, it is one item in a staged demonstration of death, preservation, and revival. The adjacent challenge about giving life (2:258) and Abraham's request to see how the dead are revived (2:260) frame the beverage as evidence within a lesson about divine power.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B011` (absorbing as understanding) is retained. It supplements the physical beverage with a pedagogic role: what is drink is also something the observer must mentally take in.
records.activation_trace: The repeated command `فَٱنظُرْ ... وَٱنظُرْ ... وَٱنظُرْ`, followed by `فَلَمَّا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ` and `أَعْلَمُ`, supplies an unusually dense cognition sequence around the target. B011 supplies the role of grasping or absorbing; the abductive bridge is that the preserved drink is explicitly presented to sight until understanding results. The target therefore contributes an evidentiary intake while remaining an ordinary beverage.
records.negative_evidence: The later mention of wealth and seed in 2:261 is too remote and indirect to activate the wealth-consumption or watered-growth branches. No container, allotment, or water quality is specified.

records.primary_reading: `شَرَابٌ` denotes the drink assigned to the condemned, specified immediately as being from scalding water.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Something ordinarily restorative is folded into the consequence of self-earned captivity. The movement from playful immersion in false discourse (6:68, 6:70) to helpless exposure, painful drink, and final gathering (6:72) turns consumption into recompense rather than relief.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: `مِنْ حَمِيمٍ` and `عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ` intensify the ordinary drink but do not activate another branch of ش ر ب. The captivity, accounting, and reversal cues alter the ayah-level force of the drink without supplying a root-local allotment, social-drinker, absorption, vessel, or understanding construction.
records.negative_evidence: `بِمَا كَسَبُوا` establishes deserved consequence, not the B010 idiom of charging someone with what was not done. The text gives no visible cue for any other ش ر ب branch.

records.primary_reading: `حَمِيمٍ` qualifies the drink as intensely hot or scalding water.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The hot liquid appears precisely where every protective relation has failed. The next ayah depicts companions calling a bewildered person toward guidance, sharpening the contrast between available human voices and the absence of an effective protector at judgment.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000001/B011` (near kin or intimate friend) is retained contrastively. The scalding water remains hot water, while the cognate branch makes it a grim substitute at the point where intimacy and aid are explicitly denied.
records.activation_trace: `لَيْسَ لَهَا ... وَلِىٌّ وَلَا شَفِيعٌ` supplies the absence of protective closeness, and `لَهُ أَصْحَابٌ يَدْعُونَهُ` in 6:71 supplies the nearby companion image. B011 contributes the role of the intimate helper; the abductive bridge is the placement of `حَمِيم` after the denial of every such relation. The target's heat thus carries an added relational deprivation: instead of a حميم who helps, there is حميم to drink.
records.negative_evidence: The binding consequences could broadly suggest fate, but no ح م م form or precise fate/death construction independently activates B010 strongly enough. Blackness, fever, bathhouse, animal, and sound branches lack local triggers.

records.primary_reading: The imperative permits people to drink, paired with eating, while forbidding excess.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The window joins bodily consumption to upright worship, adornment, pure devotion, and true versus imagined guidance. The following ayat reject invented prohibition but immediately distinguish genuinely forbidden conduct, so drinking occupies a disciplined middle between deprivation and excess.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B002` (an allotted share of water) is retained. The permission to drink is heard with the secondary contour of a bounded portion.
records.activation_trace: `وَلَا تُسْرِفُوا` is an immediate quantitative boundary on `وَٱشْرَبُوا`, while the preceding `بِٱلْقِسْطِ` (7:29) supplies a nearby norm of due measure. B002 supplies the role of an appropriate share; the bridge is that non-excess turns open permission into reception within a limit. The target therefore contributes measured enjoyment, not unrestricted intake.
records.negative_evidence: `ٱلطَّيِّبَاتِ مِنَ ٱلرِّزْقِ` supports the goodness of provision but does not specify unpleasant water, a vessel, a drinking group, or another branch. No further resonance qualifies.

records.primary_reading: The people ask Moses to obtain water for them.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Their request is answered not by a single served drink but by a durable, differentiated system: twelve peoples, twelve springs, and a known drinking-place for each. The preceding guidance and equitable dealing (7:158–159) make the water arrangement a material image of ordered guidance and justice.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B002` (providing a watering source) is retained. The request for water opens into access to continuing sources rather than a one-time handed drink.
records.activation_trace: `ٱضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ ٱلْحَجَرَ`, `ٱثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا`, and `قَدْ عَلِمَ كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ مَّشْرَبَهُمْ` visibly describe generated sources assigned for use. B002 supplies the role of durable water provision; the bridge is the narrative expansion from asking for water to receiving springs from which each group can drink. The target thus contributes infrastructure and access as well as a request for immediate relief.
records.negative_evidence: No prayer formula is quoted, so B006 is not retained merely because divine provision follows. The springs are for people rather than agricultural land, leaving B003 without its irrigation-specific trigger.

records.primary_reading: The verb depicts the twelve springs breaking or gushing out from the struck stone.
records.ayah_level_surprise: A single hard stone answers a collective need through twelve distinct outlets. The correspondence between twelve tribes and twelve springs turns emergence into an ordered distribution rather than an undifferentiated flood.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: The stone, the striking, `مِنْهُ`, the twelve `عَيْنًا`, and the following drinking-places strongly instantiate liquid breaking out through an opening, which is the verb's primary branch. None of those cues shifts the exact root toward its only other inventoried branch.
records.negative_evidence: No person is called `مُنْبَجِس` or otherwise described as abundant in good, so `root_000084/B002` is not activated. The abundant benefit of the springs is thematic resemblance, not the required person-quality construction.

records.primary_reading: `مَّشْرَبَهُمْ` is each people's known drinking-place or assigned access to water.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Knowing where to drink is embedded in a numerical correspondence between twelve tribal communities and twelve springs. The water-site is therefore also an instrument for preventing confusion and maintaining communal order.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B003` (drinkers and co-drinkers as a group) is retained. The drinking-place remains a place/share, but it also identifies a water-organized social body.
records.activation_trace: `ٱثْنَتَىْ عَشْرَةَ أَسْبَاطًا أُمَمًا`, the matched `ٱثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا`, and especially `كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ` visibly attach each source to a human collective. B003 supplies the role of a company constituted around common watering; the bridge is the one-to-one mapping of group and `مَشْرَب`. The target consequently marks social membership and coordination, not location alone.
records.negative_evidence: The branch of allotted water is already close to the noun's primary contribution here; it is not counted again as a secondary reading. No quality, absorption, vessel, or body-part cue appears.

records.primary_reading: `شَرَابٌ` is the condemned people's drink, immediately specified as scalding water.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The drink is placed inside the cosmic sequence of origin, return, and just recompense. The surrounding ordering of creation, sun, moon, years, and reckoning (10:3–6) makes even this act of consumption part of an exact moral order.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: `لِيَجْزِىَ`, `بِٱلْقِسْطِ`, and `بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ` make the drink consequential and deserved, but they do not supply a second ش ر ب image. Its punitive force remains an ayah-level reframing of ordinary drink.
records.negative_evidence: There is no bounded share, drinking company, absorption, vessel, or cognition sequence tied to this target. No secondary branch passes the standard.

records.primary_reading: `حَمِيمٍ` denotes the intensely hot water from which the punitive drink consists.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Heat is presented not as random violence but as part of a promised return and measured recompense. The surrounding regularity of created order reinforces that precision.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000001/B010` (being decreed, fated, or death close) is retained. It adds the contour of fixed adjudication to the hot water without replacing its thermal sense.
records.activation_trace: `إِلَيْهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعًا`, `وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقًّا`, `يُعِيدُهُ`, and `لِيَجْزِىَ` form an explicit sequence of certain return and decreed consequence. B010 supplies the role of unavoidable fate; the bridge is that the target appears as the concrete content of that true promise. The hot water is thus read as an enacted verdict, not merely a painful substance.
records.negative_evidence: The qualified mention of intercession in 10:3 concerns divine governance and does not visibly establish intimate kinship, so B011 is not retained. Blackness, fever, bathhouse, sound, and animal/property branches have no triggers.

records.primary_reading: The verb presents God as giving people pure milk to drink from livestock.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The drink emerges clean and easy to swallow from between waste and blood. Set after rain revives dead earth (16:65) and before fruits become drink and good provision (16:67), the act belongs to a sequence of life-giving transformations from hidden or mixed sources.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B005` (fluid gathered in the belly) is retained. The act of giving drink acquires a secondary emphasis on extracting drinkable fluid from bodily enclosure.
records.activation_trace: `مِّمَّا فِى بُطُونِهِ` is an exact bodily-container cue, and `مِنۢ بَيْنِ فَرْثٍ وَدَمٍ` identifies the difficult internal environment from which `لَبَنًا خَالِصًا` emerges. B005 supplies the role of enclosed belly-fluid; the bridge is that the liquid being given is explicitly sourced inside the animal's belly. The target consequently foregrounds transformation and release from enclosure, while still meaning that drink is given.
records.negative_evidence: The text does not describe agricultural irrigation, prayer for rain, a dyeing process, or hostility forced into a heart. Those س ق ي branches do not qualify.

records.primary_reading: The plural participle denotes the people who drink the milk, with `سَائِغًا` marking it as easy to pass or swallow.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The drinkers receive an unmingled, palatable result from a bodily setting named by waste and blood. Their ordinary act is therefore the endpoint of a concealed purification.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B005` (drinkable but unpleasant water) is retained contrastively. It does not make the milk unpleasant; it sharpens the ayah's explicit insistence that this drink is the opposite.
records.activation_trace: `لَبَنًا خَالِصًا سَائِغًا لِّلشَّارِبِينَ` places purity and effortless palatability directly against `فَرْثٍ وَدَمٍ`. B005 supplies the contrastive role of liquid that can be drunk despite disagreeable quality; the bridge is the ayah's marked removal of precisely such resistance. The target thus denotes drinkers experiencing unhindered pleasantness, not merely agents of intake.
records.negative_evidence: The plural form by itself is the ordinary designation of drinkers and is insufficient to retain the specialized co-drinker/social-watering branch B003. No allotment, vessel, absorption, or cognition cue appears.

records.primary_reading: `شَرَابٌ` is a drinkable liquid issuing from the bees, described as varied in color and containing healing for people.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The window repeatedly converts what is taken in into a new benefit: fruit is eaten, paths are followed, and a healing drink exits the belly. The next ayah's movement from knowledge to loss of knowledge places this transformation beside another contrast between hidden capacity and visible outcome.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B007` (permeating or being imbued, including color suffusion) is retained. The drink remains a liquid, but its marked colors and healing capacity make it a carrier of qualities that enter and affect.
records.activation_trace: `مُّخْتَلِفٌ أَلْوَانُهُ` is a direct color-suffusion cue, while `فِيهِ شِفَاءٌ لِّلنَّاسِ` assigns an inward effect to what the liquid carries. B007 supplies the role of a medium imbued with color and capable of permeating a recipient; the bridge is the explicit movement from varied quality within the drink to healing in people. The target therefore contributes transmission of qualities as well as potable substance.
records.negative_evidence: `يَتَفَكَّرُونَ` states the audience's reflection but does not turn drinking itself into understanding, so B011 is not retained. Nearby provision does not instantiate wealth being eaten and drunk, and no allotment or vessel is specified.

records.primary_reading: The imperative tells Mary to drink, paired with eating, as immediate bodily provision and comfort.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Eating and drinking are joined to settling the eye and then to abstaining from speech. The window moves from a stream below and ripe dates above (19:24–25) to her carrying the child before an accusing community (19:27–28), so private nourishment precedes public ordeal.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B004` (drinking place or source) and `root_000783/B014` (watered land or green growth) are retained. The command remains literal drinking while gathering the prepared location and its fruit-bearing vegetation into the act.
records.activation_trace: For B004, `قَدْ جَعَلَ رَبُّكِ تَحْتَكِ سَرِيًّا` gives an immediately local source beneath her; the branch supplies the role of a divinely arranged drinking-place, so `ٱشْرَبِى` becomes reception from a situated provision rather than an unspecified liquid. For B014, `جِذْعِ ٱلنَّخْلَةِ` and `رُطَبًا جَنِيًّا` place fresh growth beside that source; the branch supplies watered vegetation, and the bridge is the paired command `فَكُلِى وَٱشْرَبِى`, which unites fruit and water. The target consequently links nourishment from stream and plant.
records.negative_evidence: No measured portion, unpleasant water, social drinking company, absorption, or understanding construction appears. The comforting phrase `قَرِّى عَيْنًا` is not enough by itself to activate the heart-affection portion of B007.

records.primary_reading: The verb says that the messenger drinks from the same kinds of provision as his audience.
records.ayah_level_surprise: An ordinary sign of shared humanity is made into the elite's argument against obedience and resurrection. The preceding call to worship (23:32) and following warning that obedience would be loss (23:34–35) expose how common consumption is rhetorically converted into grounds for disbelief.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B003` (drinkers and co-drinkers) is retained. His drinking is made to locate him inside the audience's consuming company.
records.activation_trace: `بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ`, the parallel `يَأْكُلُ مِمَّا تَأْكُلُونَ`, and the matched `يَشْرَبُ مِمَّا تَشْرَبُونَ` insist on shared action and source. B003 supplies the role of co-drinker; the bridge is the speakers' use of common food and drink to establish social sameness. The target therefore contributes fellowship in provision, even though the speakers weaponize that fellowship against his message.
records.negative_evidence: `مِمَّا` names shared source or kind, not an assigned water-share. No vessel, quality, absorption, or cognition cue supports another branch.

records.primary_reading: The second-person plural verb denotes what the addressed people themselves drink and supplies the comparison for the messenger's drinking.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The audience's own ordinary consumption becomes the standard by which they try to disqualify a messenger. Within the window, shared embodiment is treated as incompatible with guidance even though the messenger is explicitly sent `مِّنْهُمْ` (23:32).
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B003` (drinkers and co-drinkers) is retained. The audience's drinking defines the social company into which the messenger is rhetorically placed.
records.activation_trace: `مِّنْهُمْ`, `مِثْلُكُمْ`, and the exact reciprocal parallel between his eating/drinking and `تَأْكُلُونَ`/`تَشْرَبُونَ` create a visible co-participation construction. B003 supplies the group of co-drinkers; the bridge is that “what you drink” functions as a boundary-marker of common human life. The target consequently contributes collective identity, not only the audience's act of consuming liquid.
records.negative_evidence: The plural audience alone would be insufficient, but the explicit reciprocal comparison supplies B003; no equivalent trigger exists for B002 or the remaining branches.

records.primary_reading: The noun assigns the she-camel her allotted turn or share of water.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The requested sign arrives not merely as a spectacle but as a regulated claim on a shared resource; the next two ayat make violation of that arrangement the concrete form of rejecting the sign.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B003` (drinkers and co-drinkers) is retained. It gives the she-camel's share a relational dimension: she is treated as a participant in a watering community rather than as an object that happens to consume water.
records.activation_trace: The reciprocal construction `لَّهَا شِرْبٌ وَلَكُمْ شِرْبُ` visibly sets the camel (`نَاقَةٌ`, root ن و ق) against the human plural `لَكُمْ`, while `يَوْمٍ مَّعْلُومٍ` fixes their turns. B003 supplies the category of watering participants; the bridge is that reciprocal allocation presupposes co-users of one water regime. The target consequently marks recognized participant-status as well as quantity.
records.negative_evidence: No place, vessel, unpleasant-water quality, permeation, rope, neck-extension, false charge, understanding, expenditure, waterskin, or watered-land cue is present; those branches are not retained.

records.primary_reading: The noun assigns the addressed people their known day's allotted turn or share of water.
records.ayah_level_surprise: A temporal entitlement becomes an ethical test: the following prohibition and their hamstringing of the camel show that honoring the sign means accepting a limit on communal access.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B003` (drinkers and co-drinkers) is retained. It makes the people's share define them as one party within a reciprocal watering relationship, not as unrestricted owners of the supply.
records.activation_trace: Plural `لَكُمْ`, paired with singular `لَّهَا`, and the scheduling phrase `يَوْمٍ مَّعْلُومٍ` are the visible triggers. B003 contributes the social role of a group of drinkers/co-waterers; the abductive bridge is that named alternating turns organize distinct users around the same resource. The target thus carries obligation toward the other participant along with entitlement.
records.negative_evidence: The window supplies no concrete trigger for B004-B014, and actual imbibing under B001 is entailed by the ordinary water-share sense rather than a surprising secondary activation.

records.primary_reading: The plural denotes drinks obtained from the livestock, alongside their other benefits.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The sequence moves from human ownership and control of livestock to dependence on what those animals yield, then exposes the mismatch between received benefit and the taking of powerless gods.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B012` (wealth consumed in feeding and watering) is retained. It presents the drinks not only as liquids but as the consumable yield of owned living wealth.
records.activation_trace: `أَنْعَٰمًا ... مَٰلِكُونَ` supplies livestock and property, while `فَمِنْهَا رَكُوبُهُمْ وَمِنْهَا يَأْكُلُونَ` joins use, eating, and the subsequent `مَشَارِبُ`. B012's role is economic/provisionary. Because the window explicitly frames the animals as property whose yield is eaten and drunk, the branch bridges livestock wealth to sustenance expended for its owners; the target becomes evidence of endowed provision and hence sharpens `أَفَلَا يَشْكُرُونَ`.
records.negative_evidence: Plural beneficiaries alone do not establish B003, and no drinking place, vessel, bad-water quality, permeation, fastening, or technical land/waterskin image is visible.

records.primary_reading: The participle identifies those enjoying the white, pleasurable drink passed around from a flowing source.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Pleasure is defined by subtraction as well as presence: the following ayah denies both an intrinsic affliction in the drink and depletion of the drinkers, so delight is inseparable from preserved faculties.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B005` (drinkable but unpleasant water) is retained contrastively. The window invokes that branch's compromised-drink profile only to reverse it: this drink is pleasant and consequence-free.
records.activation_trace: `لَذَّةٍ` directly supplies pleasant taste, while `لَا فِيهَا غَوْلٌ وَلَا هُمْ عَنْهَا يُنزَفُونَ` explicitly negates harm and depletion. B005 serves as a rejected quality-frame; the abductive bridge is that these unusually explicit assurances answer the category of liquid that can be drunk despite defect or aversion. The target therefore denotes drinkers whose drinking involves neither forced tolerance nor bodily loss.
records.negative_evidence: `كَأْسٍ` and `مَعِينٍ` belong to the primary serving scene and do not by themselves activate the broader locative B004; color alone does not establish permeation under B007. No other branch has a local trigger.

records.primary_reading: The noun denotes cool water to drink, paired with water for washing.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Relief from Job's bodily distress is enacted through one immediately indicated water provision with outward and inward uses; restoration then expands into restored family, mercy, memory, and vindicated patience.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B004` (drinking place or source) is retained. It makes the drink part of an encountered water-site, not merely a detached serving of liquid.
records.activation_trace: The bodily command `ٱرْكُضْ بِرِجْلِكَ`, demonstrative `هَٰذَا`, and paired predicate `مُغْتَسَلٌ بَارِدٌ وَشَرَابٌ` visibly locate wash and drink in the result of the commanded strike. B004 supplies source/place as its role. The bridge is that a deictically presented provision serving both bathing and drinking behaves as a water source; the target consequently participates in a total restorative environment, internal as well as external.
records.negative_evidence: Coolness does not activate unpleasant-water B005, and bodily restoration alone is insufficient for permeation B007 or the throat-part branch B006. No other branch passes the local-trigger standard.

records.primary_reading: The noun denotes drink requested and enjoyed with abundant fruit by the reclining inhabitants of the gardens.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The drink belongs to an order of unimpeded access: opened gates, repose, abundant requested fruit, companionship, and a promised outcome on the day of account.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: `يَدْعُونَ ... بِفَٰكِهَةٍ كَثِيرَةٍ وَشَرَابٍ` anchors plentiful feast-service and strengthens the primary reading, but it does not visibly introduce a distinct branch of ش ر ب.
records.negative_evidence: There is no explicit property/expenditure frame for B012, no source or vessel for B004, no group-of-waterers construction for B003, and no bad-water, permeation, restraint, or technical cue for the remaining branches.

records.primary_reading: The imperative commands the garden's people to drink freely and wholesomely as recompense for what they did.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Eating and drinking are framed simultaneously as gift and exact recompense: the Lord has given and protected them, yet each person remains bound to what was earned.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B012` (wealth consumed in feeding and watering) is retained. It recasts drinking as participation in provision being expended on a feast.
records.activation_trace: The exact pair `كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟`, preceded by `بِمَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ`, visibly combines eating, drinking, and bestowed provision; `بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ` gives that provision a recompensive frame. B012 contributes the image of resources used to feed and water people. The bridge is that bestowed reward is now consumed through the paired acts named by that branch, so the target registers reception of an apportioned bounty, not thirst-quenching alone.
records.negative_evidence: The plural audience does not by itself activate the co-drinker branch, and the window has no source, vessel, defective water, absorption, fastening, or other specific branch trigger.

records.primary_reading: The participle states that, after filling their bellies with zaqqum, they will drink scalding water upon it.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The sequence mimics hospitality through food, drink, and `نُزُلُهُمْ`, but every stage compounds need and punishment; drinking does not relieve the meal but extends it into the thirst-pattern of `ٱلْهِيمِ`.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B005` (drinkable but unpleasant water) is retained in an intensified form. It makes the act necessity-drinking of a liquid whose aversive quality is foregrounded.
records.activation_trace: The object `ٱلْحَمِيمِ` (root ح م م, hot water), the prior filling with `زَقُّومٍ`, and the following simile `شُرْبَ ٱلْهِيمِ` visibly combine a hostile liquid with unrelieved thirst. B005 contributes compelled tolerance of bad water. The bridge is that the drinkers continue consuming precisely what should repel them because their thirst drives them; the target becomes punitive compulsion rather than refreshment.
records.negative_evidence: Repetition of plural drinking does not by itself establish a distinct co-drinker relation, and there is no share, place, absorption, rope, or other local trigger for the remaining ش ر ب branches.

records.primary_reading: The noun denotes intensely hot or scalding water drunk as punishment.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Its placement after belly-filling and before drinking like the thirsty `هِيم` makes heat function as an anti-refreshment: the offered liquid magnifies the need it appears to answer.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: `فَشَٰرِبُونَ`, `شُرْبَ ٱلْهِيمِ`, and the meal sequence make the hot-water sense of حميم concrete, but these are effects and uses of B002's primary hot-water image rather than triggers for another ح م م branch.
records.negative_evidence: The window contains no blackness/charcoal, rendered fat, sweat or summer rain, fever, approaching event, animal sound, pursuit, explicit decree or death, intimate friend, bird, bathhouse, or choice-property cue. `يَوْمَ ٱلدِّينِ` alone is too broad to retain B010.

records.primary_reading: The plural participle states that the punished people drink, with the following cognate phrase specifying their manner as the drinking of desperately thirsty `هِيم`.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Their repeated drinking follows full bellies and hot water yet provides no satiety; the meal's apparent abundance is exposed as a cycle of compulsive excess.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B005` (drinkable but unpleasant water) is retained. It casts the drinkers as consuming an aversive liquid under necessity rather than choosing a beverage.
records.activation_trace: The preceding object `ٱلْحَمِيمِ` gives the liquid's hostile heat, and `شُرْبَ ٱلْهِيمِ` gives the visible model of pathological or extreme thirst. B005 supplies necessity-drinking despite bad quality; the bridge is that the simile explains why the group drinks the scalding liquid. The target therefore marks loss of control and failed relief.
records.negative_evidence: The plural form is already the primary designation of the drinkers and does not, without reciprocal watering roles, justify B003. No other branch has a visible trigger.

records.primary_reading: The cognate noun specifies the mode or intensity of their drinking: they drink as the thirst-driven `هِيم` drink.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The measure of punishment is not a fixed portion but an appetite that cannot complete itself, turning the usual relief of drinking into the image of endless need.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B005` (drinkable but unpleasant water) is retained. It adds the liquid-quality and necessity dimension to the target's primary manner-of-drinking sense.
records.activation_trace: `مِنَ ٱلْحَمِيمِ` in the immediately preceding ayah identifies the aversive hot liquid, while the governing `فَشَٰرِبُونَ` and comparison `ٱلْهِيمِ` make compelled, excessive intake visible. B005's role is the tolerated-but-hostile drink. The bridge is that a thirst likened to the `هِيم` accounts for consuming such water; the target comes to describe necessity overriding aversion, not quantity alone.
records.negative_evidence: The adjacent plural participles are drinking vocabulary but supply no reciprocal co-watering relation for B003; they therefore do not meet the secondary-evidence standard. No share, source, vessel, permeation, or other branch cue appears.

records.primary_reading: The imperative commands wholesome drinking in the high garden as reward for deeds sent ahead in former days.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The low-hanging fruit and immediate feast convert what was previously `أَسْلَفْتُمْ` into present enjoyment, while the abrupt next scene of the left-handed recipient shows the account's opposite outcome.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B012` (wealth consumed in feeding and watering) is retained. It makes drinking the realization and consumption of provision corresponding to what the recipients advanced beforehand.
records.activation_trace: The exact pair `كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟` activates B012's eating-and-drinking expenditure image, while `بِمَآ أَسْلَفْتُمْ` and the following `حِسَابِيَهْ` create a visible advance/account frame. B012 supplies the role of provision spent in feeding and watering. The abductive bridge is that former advances return as a feast now consumed; the target thus participates in a recompensive economy rather than denoting liquid intake alone.
records.negative_evidence: The garden and near fruit do not establish a drinking site under B004, and no defective-water, co-waterer, permeation, fastening, or technical cue supports another branch.

records.primary_reading: The verb states that the righteous drink from a cup whose mixture is camphor, in contrast to the punishment prepared for the ungrateful.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The cup is not an isolated luxury: the next ayah opens it into a spring that God's servants drink from and cause to flow, while the preceding chains and shackles sharpen the contrast between freely handled provision and imposed restraint.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B007` (permeating or being imbued), `root_000783/B004` (drinking place or source), and contrastive `root_000783/B008` (putting a rope around the neck) are retained. B007 makes the drink an imbued mixture; B004 connects the cup to an accessible, flowing source; B008 makes drinking a sign of unbound reception against the fettered alternative.
records.activation_trace: For B007, `كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا كَافُورًا` explicitly names mixture (`مِزَاج`, root م ز ج), providing the physical blending bridge by which the cup is permeated with camphor; the target becomes drinking a deliberately infused composition. For B004, the continuation `عَيْنًا يَشْرَبُ بِهَا ... يُفَجِّرُونَهَا تَفْجِيرًا` names a spring (`عَيْنًا`, root ع ي ن) and its caused flowing (`ف ج ر`); the appositional continuation bridges cup to source, so the target includes access to and agency over the drink's fountain. For B008, the immediately preceding `سَلَٰسِلَا۟ وَأَغْلَٰلًا` supplies literal chains and neck-fetters in the explicit unbeliever/righteous contrast; B008's fastening image is abductively reversed, so the target's bodily reception of the cup signals freedom from imposed binding.
records.negative_evidence: B008 is retained only because the local fetter language and contrast are unusually explicit; no animal-rope scenario is asserted as the primary sense. The window gives no water-share, unpleasant-water, neck-craning, false-charge, understanding, wealth-expenditure, waterskin, or watered-land trigger.

records.primary_reading: The verb contributes literal drinking: God's servants drink from a spring which they cause to flow abundantly.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The spring stands between the chains, shackles, and blaze prepared for rejecters (76:4) and the servants' fulfillment, fear, and feeding of the needy, orphan, and captive (76:7-8). Their reception of freely flowing drink is thus paired with their own giving rather than mere consumption.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B008` (putting a rope around the neck) is activated contrastively: drinking at the spring becomes bodily freedom opposite the neck-restraint image in the window. `root_000783/B007` (permeating or being imbued) is also retained: the drink can echo an inward saturation by the `حُبّ` that accompanies their feeding, without displacing the literal drink.
records.activation_trace: For B008, `سَلَاسِلَا وَأَغْلَالًا` in 76:4 supplies an unusually exact restraint trigger; the bridge is the contrast between forced binding at the neck and servants approaching and channeling their drink, which makes `يَشْرَبُ` a sign of uncoerced access. For B007, `عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِ` in 76:8 supplies the heart-affection cue named in that branch; the bridge is that what enters as drink corresponds to an affection that has entered deeply enough to issue in feeding others, adding inward formation to bodily refreshment.
records.negative_evidence: The plural drinkers in 76:5 and the collective `عِبَادُ ٱللَّهِ` do not by themselves establish B003; they remain ordinary participants in literal drinking. No allotment, vessel, unpleasant-water, understanding, or technical waterskin cue supports the other branches.

records.primary_reading: The verb presents their Lord as the direct giver of a purifying drink.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The ayah moves from visible clothing and silver adornment to a drink given by the Lord, then identifies the whole scene as recompense for acknowledged effort (76:22). Exterior honor is completed by an interior gift.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B009` (soaking cloth with dye) is retained. It makes the divine giving of drink echo the saturation of the richly colored clothing immediately before it, so the drink reads as an inward counterpart to the wearers' outward coloration.
records.activation_trace: `ثِيَابُ سُندُسٍ خُضْرٌ وَإِسْتَبْرَقٌ` supplies both the cloth and color cues required by B009. The bridge is the ayah's direct sequence from colored garments to `سَقَىٰهُمْ`; the branch contributes an image of saturation, and `طَهُورًا` directs that image toward permeating purity rather than merely quenching thirst.
records.negative_evidence: `جَزَاءً` establishes reward but not specifically a water-share, so B003 is not retained. There is no rain-prayer, cloud, belly-fluid, or forced-heart cue for B005-B007 or B010.

records.primary_reading: The noun denotes the actual drink given by their Lord, expressly characterized as purifying.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The drink is the inward member of a sequence otherwise dominated by things seen and worn: green silk, brocade, and silver bracelets. The next ayah makes this embodied transformation a recompense for their striving.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B007` (permeating or being imbued) is retained. The drink is not recast as metaphorical; rather, its literal ingestion acquires the additional suggestion that purity permeates the drinkers as color permeates their garments.
records.activation_trace: The immediate `ثِيَابُ ... خُضْرٌ` provides B007's visible color-suffusion image, while `طَهُورًا` gives the quality proposed to permeate. The bridge is the outer-to-inner sequence in one ayah; this changes `شَرَابًا` from an item of refreshment alone into the medium of an inwardly absorbed purity.
records.negative_evidence: Neither the nearby reward language nor the attendants establishes an allotted water-turn or a company of co-drinkers. No vessel, unpleasant-water, neck, understanding, or waterskin trigger is visible.

records.primary_reading: The imperative grants the addressees literal, wholesome enjoyment of drink alongside food.
records.ayah_level_surprise: What they desire in the preceding fruit scene is immediately tied to what they used to do, and the next ayah names this as recompense for those who do good. Appetite is not detached indulgence here but enjoyment proportioned to action, against the following warning to deniers.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B002` (an allotted share of water) is retained. It adds to the imperative the sense of an assigned, fitting portion within recompense, while the commanded act remains literal drinking.
records.activation_trace: `بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ` and `كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ` visibly construe the food and drink as a consequence and award. The abductive bridge is from recompense proportioned to deeds to B002's allotted portion; this makes `ٱشْرَبُوا` reception of one's granted share, not merely permission to consume.
records.negative_evidence: `هَنِيئًا` confirms pleasant literal drinking and therefore does not activate the unpleasant-water branch B005. The collective imperative alone is insufficient for B003, and no vessel, color-permeation, restraint, or understanding cue appears.

records.primary_reading: The noun denotes drink as a denied form of relief: the inhabitants taste neither coolness nor drink there, apart from the named exceptions in 78:25.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Prolonged residence (78:23) does not normalize the place into habitation; even the paired basics of coolness and drink are negated. The exception `حَمِيمًا وَغَسَّاقًا`, followed by `جَزَاءً وِفَاقًا`, turns what should relieve into an exact mode of recompense.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B005` (drinkable but unpleasant water) is retained. The branch sharpens the exception: “drink” survives only at the threshold of something ingestible while its ordinary promise of refreshment is stripped away.
records.activation_trace: The construction `لَا ... شَرَابًا إِلَّا حَمِيمًا وَغَسَّاقًا` is the local trigger: categorical denial is followed by two explicitly named substitutes, one directly paired with the denied drink. The bridge to B005 is necessity-level or aversive drinkability; this changes `شَرَابًا` into a bitter category boundary rather than a benefit.
records.negative_evidence: The window has no share, drinking company, vessel, color, restraint, understanding, or vegetation cue. The duration and recompense language affect the ayah-level force but do not activate another branch of ش ر ب.

records.primary_reading: The verb contributes literal drinking by those brought near from the spring associated with the preceding mixture of Tasnim.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The invitation to compete in the sealed, musk-finished drink (83:26) resolves not in possession of an object but in proximity to a source. The following mockery of believers (83:29-30) makes that true nearness answer a social order that had treated them as laughable.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000783/B004` (drinking place or source) is retained. It makes the act of drinking carry locative force: the privilege of the brought-near is direct relation to the spring, not only receipt of its liquid.
records.activation_trace: `عَيْنًا` and the construction `يَشْرَبُ بِهَا` visibly present a source with which the drinking is performed, while `ٱلْمُقَرَّبُونَ` emphasizes proximity. The bridge is from proximity to a named spring to B004's drinking-place/source branch; the added contribution is access at the source itself.
records.negative_evidence: `فَلْيَتَنَافَسِ` can suggest aspiration but does not establish an allotted water-turn, so B002 is not retained. The plural subject is not enough for B003, and no unpleasantness, permeation, restraint, or understanding cue appears.

records.primary_reading: In the negated clause, the verb describes agricultural watering: the specified cow has not been used to irrigate cultivated land, just as it has not been trained to stir the earth.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The questioners' repeated concern with identifying appearance in 2:69-70 culminates in a description joining labor history to an unmarked body. Only after color, work, and marking have all been specified do they claim that the truth has arrived, yet they nearly fail to act.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B009` (soaking cloth with dye) is retained contrastively. The negated irrigation sits inside a window saturated with color language, so `تَسْقِى` participates secondarily in an image of something neither worked by water nor marked by color.
records.activation_trace: `لَوْنُهَا`, `صَفْرَاءُ فَاقِعٌ لَّوْنُهَا` (2:69), and `لَا شِيَةَ فِيهَا` (2:71) provide repeated visible color and marking triggers for B009. The bridge is the shared saturation pattern between watering and dye-soaking; because both irrigation and marking are negated or excluded around the cow, the target additionally helps portray an untouched specimen rather than only an animal lacking one farm task.
records.negative_evidence: B003's irrigation share/channel domain belongs to the ordinary agricultural reading here and is not counted as a secondary resonance. No belly-fluid, rain-prayer, rain-cloud, reed, or forced-heart cue is visible.

records.primary_reading: The noun denotes the service of giving drink to pilgrims, placed alongside maintenance of the Sacred Mosque.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The window first contrasts two kinds of mosque association (9:17-18), then refuses to equate conspicuous sanctuary service with faith and striving. The following ayat relocate greatness and success from public function to committed expenditure of wealth and self.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: `سِقَايَةَ ٱلْحَاجِّ` supplies a recipient and therefore securely establishes the ordinary giving-drink service, but no further local form or image bridges it to another listed branch.
records.negative_evidence: The religious setting does not itself activate B006's specific prayer for water or rain. Mosque maintenance is not an irrigation channel, and the window contains no belly fluid, rain cloud, water-fed reed, dyed cloth, or forced hostility entering a heart.

records.primary_reading: The verb means that one prisoner will give or serve wine to his master.
records.ayah_level_surprise: A routine act of service is made the compact sign of one prisoner's survival and restored station, while the parallel future of the other is execution and bodily consumption. The following failed remembrance extends the contrast: the restored servant can carry wine yet does not immediately carry Joseph's request into speech.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: The explicit object `خَمْرًا` and recipient `رَبَّهُ` fully account for B001's ordinary giving-drink sense. The parallel execution, remembrance, and forgetting alter the narrative weight of the act but provide no lexical bridge to a different س ق ي branch.
records.negative_evidence: The grim neighboring outcome is insufficient for B010: no heart, repeated hostile words, or forced inward dislike appears. There is likewise no enduring water source, irrigation, belly fluid, rain prayer, cloud, reed, or dye-soaking cue.

records.primary_reading: Here the noun is a drinking vessel placed in the brother's baggage; 12:72 identifies the missing object as the king's `صُوَاع`.
records.ayah_level_surprise: An object ordinarily associated with drinking is concealed as the mechanism of a public theft accusation. The private disclosure of brotherhood in 12:69 is thus followed by a public cry that temporarily reverses protection into apparent incrimination.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: `جَعَلَ ٱلسِّقَايَةَ فِى رَحْلِ أَخِيهِ`, followed by `نَفْقِدُ صُوَاعَ ٱلْمَلِكِ`, visibly fixes the target as a portable vessel. That supports the primary reading only; it does not provide a warranted bridge to any different branch present in this packet's inventory.
records.negative_evidence: The supplied س ق ي inventory has no vessel branch, and B001 and B002 explicitly exclude the vessel called `سقاء` or `سقاية`; assigning either would violate the branch boundaries. The accusation does not by itself establish B010's repeated hostility forced into a heart, and no irrigation, belly-fluid, rain, reed, or dye cue appears.

records.primary_reading: The verb presents the addressees as being given drink from what is inside the cattle's bellies, one benefit among many that also include food.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The window moves through gardens and fruit, a tree yielding oil and `صِبْغ` for eaters, drink from inside cattle, and carriage on animals and ships. Provision repeatedly arrives by converting a concealed or rooted source into something bodily useful.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B005` (fluid gathered in the belly) and `root_000722/B009` (soaking cloth with dye) are retained. B005 foregrounds the unlikely bodily enclosure from which the drink is supplied; B009 places that supply beside the preceding image of a coloring or saturating substance, adding material absorption to the sequence of benefits.
records.activation_trace: For B005, `مِّمَّا فِى بُطُونِهَا` is an exact locative trigger for enclosed bodily fluid. The bridge does not make the beneficial drink pathological; it uses the branch's enclosure image to emphasize transformation from hidden belly-fluid to potable provision. For B009, `وَصِبْغٍ لِّلْآكِلِينَ` in 23:20 is the branch's explicit dye/color cue; the immediate sequence from `صِبْغ` to `نُّسْقِيكُم` links two forms of material saturation, making the drink one instance in a broader provision of substances that can enter and benefit bodies.
records.negative_evidence: The gardens, palms, grapes, and growing tree make irrigation imaginable but do not mention a land-share or channel, so B003 is not retained. There is no prayer for rain, rain-heavy cloud, water-fed reed, or repeated hostility entering a heart.

records.primary_reading: The form-IV verb presents the descended pure water as something God supplies for the drinking of cattle and many humans, alongside its revival of a dead town.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The window aligns `ٱلنَّوْمَ سُبَاتًا / ٱلنَّهَارَ نُشُورًا` with `بَلْدَةً مَّيْتًا / لِّنُحْۦِىَ`, then follows the distributed water with remembrance and refusal (`لِيَذَّكَّرُوا۟` / `كُفُورًا`). The supply of drink is therefore also one element in a repeated life-restoration sign, not merely relief of thirst.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B003` (the water-share of land and irrigation) is retained as a landward resonance. It makes `نُسْقِيَ` participate in sustaining the inhabited environment from which creaturely drinking cannot be detached.
records.activation_trace: The visible sequence is water descending from the sky (`أَنزَلْنَا ... مَآءً`), reviving `بَلْدَةً مَّيْتًا`, and then being apportioned `بَيْنَهُمْ` in 25:50. Those cues supply land, watering, and distribution; abductively, the same rain supplied to drinkers also constitutes a territory's irrigation share. Thus B003 changes the target from a discrete delivery to cattle and people into part of a distributed land-and-life system.
records.negative_evidence: The winds and descending water do not name a cloud, so B007 is not retained. There is no prayer formula for B006, crop/channel detail for a narrower channel reading, bodily fluid, reed, dye, or hostile speech to activate B005 or B008-B010.

records.primary_reading: The verb says that the speaker's Lord gives him drink, paired directly with giving him food.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Creation and guidance precede the food-drink pair, while illness/healing and death/life follow it. Ordinary drinking is thereby placed inside a continuous series of divine acts that spans bodily maintenance, recovery, and existence itself.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: `يُطْعِمُنِى وَيَسْقِينِ` strongly realizes ordinary giving of food and drink, but the neighboring acts expand the ayah at the level of divine care rather than visibly activating a different branch of س ق ي.
records.negative_evidence: `مَرِضْتُ` is too general to activate `root_000722/B005`, which specifically requires abnormal enclosed belly fluid. No water source or allotment, land, prayer for rain, cloud, reed, dye, or repeated hostile speech is visible.

records.primary_reading: He performs the two women's immediate watering task for them, a concrete act of service at the water of Midian.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Immediately after supplying their need, he withdraws and declares himself poor toward whatever good his Lord sends down; the next ayah makes the completed service the ground of a summons and recompense. The provider of water is thus simultaneously exposed as a recipient in need.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B002` (providing a watering source or access to it) is retained. It recasts the act from doing one task into making a blocked communal resource usable for the two women.
records.activation_trace: In 28:23 the setting is explicitly `مَآءَ مَدْيَنَ`; a crowd is watering, the women are holding back, and they say they cannot water until the shepherds depart. That congestion is a visible access problem. Abductively, his `فَسَقَىٰ لَهُمَا` resolves not only thirst but exclusion from the source, so B002 adds restored access to the primary act.
records.negative_evidence: The prayer beginning `رَبِّ` asks for `خَيْرٍ`, not water or rain, so it does not meet the burden for B006. No land/crop/channel, bodily fluid, rain cloud, reed, dye, or hostile-heart construction activates B003 or B005-B010.

records.primary_reading: `مَا سَقَيْتَ لَنَا` identifies his completed watering service for the two women as the act for which their father intends to repay him.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The small water service becomes `أَجْر`, then prompts the assessment `ٱلْقَوِىُّ ٱلْأَمِينُ` and an employment proposal. The act's contribution exceeds the water delivered: it publicly discloses character and opens a durable social arrangement.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B002` (providing a watering source or access) is retained. The recalled act carries the additional sense that he enabled the women to use a source from which the crowd had effectively held them back.
records.activation_trace: The window begins with `مَآءَ مَدْيَنَ`, the watering crowd, the women's `تَذُودَانِ`, and their inability to water until the shepherds leave. The focus phrase then converts that access-restoring deed into a compensated service. The abductive bridge is that overcoming the source barrier amounts to providing usable watering access; B002 therefore makes `سَقَيْتَ` socially enabling, not only manually helpful.
records.negative_evidence: Neither payment nor employment activates another water sense. There is no rain prayer, cultivated land or channel, bodily fluid, cloud, reed, dye, or hostile speech for B003 or B005-B010.

records.primary_reading: The passive verb presents the inhabitants as being served a cup whose mixture is ginger.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The cup is framed by silver vessels and precisely measured glassware before it, a named spring after it, and circulating attendants after that. Drinking appears as an ordered environment of prepared abundance rather than an isolated beverage.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B002` (provision of a watering source) is retained. It extends the served cup into continuing provision grounded in a spring within the same realm.
records.activation_trace: The immediate continuation `عَيْنًا فِيهَا تُسَمَّىٰ سَلْسَبِيلًا` visibly supplies a resident spring, while `وَيُطَافُ عَلَيْهِم` and the vessels establish service. Abductively, the cup is not a one-off draught but an offered drink backed by an available source; B002 changes the target from cup-service alone to source-secured provision.
records.negative_evidence: `مِزَاجُهَا` names a beverage mixture, not cloth saturated with dye, so B009 is not activated. Nothing indicates agricultural irrigation, belly fluid, prayer for rain, a rain cloud, reed growth, or hostile speech.

records.primary_reading: The passive verb says that they are served from a sealed pure drink.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Facial radiance precedes the drink; its seal, musk, invitation to compete, and blend from Tasnim follow it. The serving therefore marks recognized felicity and an object of aspiration, not merely consumption.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: The seal, scent, competition, and mixture richly qualify the beverage and its status, but none supplies the defining construction of another س ق ي branch.
records.negative_evidence: `مِزَاجُهُۥ مِن تَسْنِيمٍ` gives the blend's provenance but does not visibly establish an at-will watering source or allotment for B002. There is no land/channel, bodily fluid, rain prayer or cloud, reed, dyed cloth, or forced hostile speech.

records.primary_reading: The passive verb depicts the suffering figure as made to drink from a fiercely hot spring.
records.ayah_level_surprise: A blazing fire precedes the hot spring, and food that neither fattens nor relieves hunger follows it. Drink and food retain their ordinary bodily roles only to reverse them: the whole sustenance sequence intensifies deprivation.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: `نَارًا حَامِيَةً`, `عَيْنٍ ءَانِيَةٍ`, and the nutritionally useless food create a strong punitive contrast, but this remains an ayah-level inversion of nourishment rather than a separately grounded س ق ي branch.
records.negative_evidence: Although `عَيْنٍ` is a source, coerced drinking from a harmful spring is not the usable, at-will provision required by B002. B010 would require disliked words or hostility repeatedly forced into the heart; no speech, heart, or repetition appears. The window also lacks land irrigation, belly fluid, prayer, cloud, reed, and dye.

records.primary_reading: The noun denotes the she-camel's protected watering entitlement or allotted access, coupled with the warning to leave God's she-camel inviolate.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The compact warning is immediately followed by denial, hamstringing, and comprehensive punishment. Respect for a creature's water entitlement becomes the concrete boundary whose violation exposes the community's wider rebellion.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_000722/B001` (giving drink to a drinker) is retained. It keeps the allotted `سُقْيَٰهَا` tied to the actual act of satisfying a living animal's need.
records.activation_trace: The explicit `نَاقَةَ` immediately precedes the coordinated possessive `وَسُقْيَٰهَا`; the drinker and her watering are thus visibly joined. Abductively, an allotment matters because it must issue in the camel actually receiving drink. B001 changes the target from an abstract schedule or right into embodied care for its beneficiary.
records.negative_evidence: No cultivated land, crop, or channel supports B003, and there is no prayer formula for B006. The window supplies no belly fluid, cloud, reed, dye, or hostile-heart idiom.

records.primary_reading: God causes those who denied the signs to drown, in direct contrast to saving the messenger and those with him in the ark.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The preceding appeal presents warning, guardedness, and hoped-for mercy; the focus ayah then resolves response into the sharp spatial opposition of safety `فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ` and drowning. The next ayah opens another people's address, so the event also closes one failed reception and clears the narrative field for another.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_001080/B002` (killing by drowning) is retained. It makes the causative drowning not merely submersion but the terminal execution of judgment upon the deniers.
records.activation_trace: The same clause contrasts causative rescue (`فَأَنجَيْنَٰهُ`) with causative drowning (`وَأَغْرَقْنَا`) and identifies the latter group twice by denial. Because rescue and its lethal opposite partition the two groups, the abductive bridge to B002 is explicit intentional death by water. The branch makes finality and judicial discrimination part of the target's contribution.
records.negative_evidence: No tear-filled eye or saturated land activates B003, no bow activates B004, and no whole-scope absorption language activates B005. The later `ٱلْمَلَأُ` is a social designation in a new episode, not an image of an eye or land filled with water; milk-draught and ornamental-bridle branches also lack triggers.

records.primary_reading: God causes them to drown in the great water as vengeance for denying and neglecting the signs.
records.ayah_level_surprise: They first promise faith and release if the affliction is removed, then break that promise. After their drowning, the formerly weakened people inherit the eastern and western lands, the old works are destroyed, and they cross `ٱلْبَحْرَ`; the water event thus becomes a reversal of mastery, barring one group where another passes onward.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_001080/B002` (killing by drowning) and `root_001080/B005` (whole-scope immersion or absorption) are retained. B002 makes the drowning the deliberately lethal act of vengeance; B005 makes it the pivot of a comprehensive removal and transfer, not only the loss of bodies.
records.activation_trace: For B002, `فَٱنتَقَمْنَا مِنْهُمْ فَأَغْرَقْنَٰهُمْ` directly links intentional vengeance to causative drowning, while `بِأَنَّهُمْ` supplies the stated ground. For B005, 7:137 visibly totalizes the aftermath with `مَشَٰرِقَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَغَٰرِبَهَا` and `دَمَّرْنَا مَا كَانَ يَصْنَعُ ... وَمَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْرِشُونَ`. Abductively, the drowning opens into the absorption of the former order's entire territorial and constructed scope; this adds regime-wide displacement to the primary water death.
records.negative_evidence: No eye filled with tears or land saturated by irrigation supports B003, and there is no bow, small milk draught, or ornamented bridle for B004, B006, or B008.

records.primary_reading: `ٱلْيَمِّ` denotes the sea or great body of water in which the drowning occurs.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The following window calls the crossed water `ٱلْبَحْرَ`: the same narrative span makes great water the place of one group's destruction and another group's successful passage. The yamm is therefore a boundary that discriminates outcomes, not neutral scenery.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: The vengeance sequence makes the drowning purposeful, but purpose belongs to `فَٱنتَقَمْنَا` and the causative `فَأَغْرَقْنَٰهُمْ`; it does not visibly turn the noun `ٱلْيَمِّ` into an act of aiming. The sea/great-water branch remains primary without supplementation.
records.negative_evidence: `root_001697/B001` is explicitly distinct in origin from the sea branch and lacks a form or construction of قصد, تعمد, seeking, or singling out; general intentionality cannot meet that higher burden. There is no clean earth, face/hand wiping, or prayer context for B002, and no bird or Yamamah proper-name cue for B004-B005.

records.primary_reading: The verb states that God caused Pharaoh's house to drown; it supplies the concrete watery mode of their destruction while leaving the preceding statement of destruction intact.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The doubled comparison with earlier rejecters (8:52, 8:54), the intervening account of a favor changing only after inward change (8:53), and the repeated covenant-breaking of 8:56 make the drowning one instance of a recurring moral pattern rather than an isolated disaster.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_001080/B002` is retained: beside drowning as the mode, the verb participates in the ayah's broader language of killing or destruction. `root_001080/B005` is also retained: the result is presented as exhaustive, absorbing the whole implicated collective.
records.activation_trace: For B002, the immediately coordinated `فَأَهْلَكْنَٰهُم ... وَأَغْرَقْنَا` explicitly pairs destruction with drowning; the bridge is that the second action specifies and intensifies the first, so the target contributes both submersion and lethal eradication. For B005, `وَكُلٌّ كَانُوا۟ ظَٰلِمِينَ`, reinforced by `كُلِّ مَرَّةٍ` in 8:56, makes totality conspicuous; the bridge from the branch's whole-scope absorption is that no part of the culpable pattern escapes, so the drowning reads as comprehensive judgment on Pharaoh's house.
records.negative_evidence: No tear-filled eye or saturated land activates B003, no bow or extreme draw activates B004, no draught activates B006, and no ornamented bridle activates B008.

records.primary_reading: The verb says that God drowned those who denied the signs, in contrast to saving Noah and his companions in the ark.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The drowning is framed by a transfer of historical position: those aboard are not merely rescued but made successors, while the reader is ordered to inspect the deniers' outcome. The water event thus marks both removal and succession.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: `فَنَجَّيْنَٰهُ ... فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ` supplies a sharp rescue/drowning contrast, and `جَعَلْنَٰهُمْ خَلَٰٓئِفَ` supplies the succession dimension, but these clarify the primary event rather than activate a distinct غ ر ق branch.
records.negative_evidence: The lethal consequence of drowning and the command `فَأَجْمِعُوا۟ أَمْرَكُمْ` are insufficient by themselves for B002 or B005: the former adds no independent killing cue, and the latter concerns assembling a plan rather than whole-scope immersion. There is no local trigger for B003, B004, B006, or B008.

records.primary_reading: The passive participle declares the wrongdoers destined to be drowned, explaining why Noah must build the ark and must not plead concerning them.
records.ayah_level_surprise: The window juxtaposes divinely supervised making (`بِأَعْيُنِنَا وَوَحْيِنَا`) with the people's mockery of that making in 11:38. The drowning is therefore already certain while its visible means is still being ridiculed.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: The ark, the prohibition on intercession, and the ensuing `عَذَابٌ` make the primary drowning a fixed punitive outcome, but none supplies an independent image belonging to another غ ر ق branch.
records.negative_evidence: `عَذَابٌ` does not independently establish B002's broader killing sense, and `كُلَّمَا` is distributive timing rather than B005's whole-field absorption. The window has no tears or saturated land (B003), bow-draw (B004), draught (B006), or ornamented bridle (B008).

records.primary_reading: The verb presents a possible divine causing-to-drown at sea through a violent wind, in consequence of disbelief.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Rescue from sea to land in 17:67 is followed by insecurity even on land in 17:68 and the possibility of being returned to sea in 17:69. Against 17:70's honoring and carrying of Adam's children on land and sea, the same domains of transport and favor remain domains of accountability.
records.water_root_resonance: No secondary water-root branch retained.
records.activation_trace: `ٱلْبَحْرِ`, `قَاصِفًا مِّنَ ٱلرِّيحِ`, and the prior `نَجَّىٰكُمْ` visibly anchor literal drowning and its reversal; `لَا تَجِدُوا۟ ... تَبِيعًا` adds the absence of recourse, not a second غ ر ق sense.
records.negative_evidence: Neither the danger's lethality nor the storm independently triggers B002; no expression of exhaustive absorption supports B005. B003, B004, B006, and B008 lack their required local images.

records.primary_reading: The verb reports that God drowned Pharaoh and those with him after Pharaoh sought to dislodge the Israelites from the land.
records.ayah_level_surprise: Pharaoh's intended removal from the land is reversed: he and his company are removed, while the Israelites are told in 17:104 to dwell in that same land. Drowning becomes the hinge of a territorial reversal.
records.water_root_resonance: `root_001080/B005` is retained: the drowning takes in the whole opposing company and clears the field they had tried to control.
